                                                                                                                                              • SkillOS: Learning Skill Curation for Self-Evolving Agents

                                                                                                                                              SkillOS：为自我进化智能体学习技能编排

                                                                                                                                              Siru Ouyang 1∗ ,Jun Yan 2† ,Yanfei Chen 2 ,Rujun Han 2 ,Zifeng Wang 2 ,Bhavana Dalvi Mishra 2 ,Rui Meng 2 , Chun-Liang Li 2 , Yizhu Jiao 1 , Kaiwen Zha 3 , Maohao Shen 3 , Vishy Tirumalashetty 2 , George Lee 2 , Jiawei Han 1 , Tomas Pfister 2 and Chen-Yu Lee 2† Siru Ouyang 1∗ ,Jun Yan 2† ,Yanfei Chen 2 ,Rujun Han 2 ,Zifeng Wang 2 ,Bhavana Dalvi Mishra 2 ,Rui Meng 2 , Chun-Liang Li 2 , Yizhu Jiao 1 , Kaiwen Zha 3 , Maohao Shen 3 , Vishy Tirumalashetty 2 , George Lee 2 , Jiawei Han 1 , Tomas Pfister 2 and Chen-Yu Lee 2†

                                                                                                                                              1 University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, 2 Google Cloud AI Research, 3 Massachusetts Institute of Technology 1 伊利诺伊大学厄巴纳-香槟分校，2 Google Cloud AI Research，3 麻省理工学院

                                                                                                                                              LLM-based agents are increasingly deployed to handle streaming tasks, yet they often remain one-off problem solvers that fail to learn from past interactions. Reusable skills distilled from experience provide a natural substrate for self-evolution, where high-quality skill curation serves as the key bottleneck. Existing approaches either rely on manual skill curation, prescribe heuristic skill operations, or train for short-horizon skill adaptation, but still struggle to learn complex long-term curation policies from indirect and delayed feedback. We propose SkillOS, an experience-driven RL training recipe for learning skill curation in self-evolving agents. SKILLOS pairs a frozen agent executor that retrieves and applies skills with a trainable skill curator that updates an external SKILLREPO from accumulated experience. To provide learning signals for curation, we train on grouped task streams based on skill-relevant task dependencies, where earlier trajectories update the SKILLREPO, and later related tasks evaluate these updates. We further design composite rewards to better attribute downstream executor feedback to curation decisions. Across multi-turn agentic tasks and single-turn reasoning tasks, SKILLOS consistently outperforms memory-free and strong memory-based baselines in both effectiveness and efficiency, with the learned skill curator generalizing across different executor backbones and task domains. Further analyses show that the learned curator produces more targeted skill use, while the evolving SKILL REPO develops richer internal structure and higher-level meta-skills over time. 基于 LLM 的智能体正日益被部署来处理流式任务，但它们往往仍只是一次性问题解决者，无法从过往交互中学习。由经验提炼而来的可复用技能为自我进化提供了天然基础，而高质量的技能编排是关键瓶颈。现有方法要么依赖人工技能编排，要么规定启发式技能操作，要么只针对短期技能适应进行训练，但仍难以从间接且延迟的反馈中学到复杂的长期编排策略。我们提出 SkillOS，这是一种面向经验驱动的强化学习训练方案，用于学习自我进化智能体中的技能编排。SKILLOS 将一个冻结的智能体执行器与一个可训练的技能编排器配对；前者检索并应用技能，后者根据积累的经验更新外部 SKILLREPO。为向编排过程提供学习信号，我们基于与技能相关的任务依赖对任务流进行分组训练：较早的轨迹更新 SKILLREPO，后续相关任务则评估这些更新。我们进一步设计了复合奖励，以更好地将下游执行器反馈归因于编排决策。在多轮智能体任务和单轮推理任务上，SKILLOS 在有效性和效率上始终优于无记忆基线和强记忆基线，且所学技能编排器能在不同执行器骨干和任务领域间泛化。进一步分析表明，所学编排器能产生更有针对性的技能使用，而不断演化的 SKILL REPO 也会随着时间发展出更丰富的内部结构和更高层次的元技能。

                                                                                                                                              1. Introduction

                                                                                                                                              1. 引言

                                                                                                                                              LLM-based agents (Wang et al., 2024) are increasingly deployed in real-world scenarios, where they must move beyond instantaneous problem-solving toward long-term proficiency (He et al., 2026b). However, the prevailing paradigm of "one-off" task execution limits their utility in streaming settings, where tasks unfold sequentially over time. This makes self-evolution (Fang et al., 2025a; Gao et al., 2025) essential: capable agents should not repeatedly start from scratch, but instead continually accumulate, refine, and reuse experience for future tasks. 基于大语言模型的智能体（Wang 等，2024）正越来越多地部署到真实场景中，此时它们必须从“即时解决问题”进一步走向长期熟练度（He 等，2026b）。然而，“一次性”任务执行范式限制了它们在流式场景中的效用：任务会在一段时间内按顺序展开。这使得自我演化（Fang 等，2025a；Gao 等，2025）变得必不可少：具备能力的智能体不应反复从零开始，而应持续积累、完善并复用经验，以用于未来任务。

                                                                                                                                              A key substrate for self-evolution is procedural memory (Fang et al., 2025b; Hu et al., 2025; Wu et al., 2025b), specifically, reusable skills (Anthropic, 2025b; Wang et al., 2025c) accumulated from past interactions. In real-world streaming settings (Wu et al., 2024), a skill-based self-evolving agent typically follows a closed-loop workflow: for each new task, it selects relevant skills, uses them to guide execution, and updates its skill collection based on the resulting trajectory. This makes skill curation-the extraction of high-quality lessons and their integration into the skill collection-essential for self-evolving agents. 自我演化的关键底座是过程性记忆（Fang 等，2025b；Hu 等，2025；Wu 等，2025b），更具体地说，是可复用技能（Anthropic，2025b；Wang 等，2025c），即从过去交互中积累的技能。在真实流式场景（Wu 等，2024）中，基于技能的自我演化智能体通常遵循闭环工作流：对每个新任务，它会选择相关技能，用这些技能来指导执行，并根据由此产生的轨迹更新其技能集合。这使得技能策划——提取高质量经验教训并集成到技能集合中——对自我演化智能体至关重要。

                                                                                                                                              However, existing skill curation works remain limited. Manually curated skills, such as Anthropic's skills repository (Anthropic, 2025b), demand huge human expertise and cannot scale to the diversity of tasks that agents may encounter. Prompting or heuristic-based methods that dictate memory operations (Qiu et al., 2025; Xu et al., 2025; Zheng et al., 2025) rely on fixed rules and lack downstream performance feedback, preventing them from adapting to the executor's actual needs. 然而，现有的技能策划工作仍有局限。诸如 Anthropic 的技能仓库（Anthropic，2025b）这类人工策划技能，需要巨量的人类专业知识，且无法扩展到智能体可能遇到的多样化任务。采用提示或基于启发式的方法来指定记忆操作（Qiu 等，2025；Xu 等，2025；Zheng 等，2025），依赖固定规则，且缺少对下游表现的反馈，因而无法适应执行器的实际需求。

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              Main contact: siruo2@illinois.edu, {junyann, chenyulee}@google.com 主要联系人：siruo2@illinois.edu，{junyann, chenyulee}@google.com

                                                                                                                                              • This work was done while Siru interned at Google Cloud AI Research. † Corresponding authors.

                                                                                                                                              • 本工作在 Siru 于 Google Cloud AI Research 实习期间完成。† 通讯作者。

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              Figure 1 | SKILLOS pairs a frozen Agent Executor with a trainable Skill Curator. The executor retrieves relevant skills from SKILLREPO to act; the curator edits the repo (insert/update/delete) based on the resulting experiences, with Markdown as the skill format. 图 1 | SKILLOS 将一个冻结的智能体执行器与一个可训练的技能策划器配对。执行器从 SKILLREPO 中检索相关技能来执行；策划器根据由此产生的经验编辑仓库（插入/更新/删除），并以 Markdown 作为技能格式。

                                                                                                                                              Recent studies explored reinforcement learning (RL) to optimize skill-based agent systems. However, they either focus on teaching agents to use skills (Tu et al., 2026; Xia et al., 2026) or optimize skill operations within a short task stream (Wang et al., 2025a; Ye et al., 2026). This limits the density of learning signals available for curating highly reusable skills and mastering complex management operations such as skill update and deletion, which are essential for robust and scalable long-term self-evolution. 近期研究探索了强化学习（RL）以优化基于技能的智能体系统。然而，它们要么关注教智能体如何使用技能（Tu 等，2026；Xia 等，2026），要么在短任务流中优化技能操作（Wang 等，2025a；Ye 等，2026）。这会限制用于策划高度可复用技能、并掌握诸如技能更新与删除等复杂管理操作的学习信号密度，而这些对于稳健且可扩展的长期自我演化至关重要。

                                                                                                                                              To tackle this challenge, we propose SkillOS, an experience-driven RL training recipe to learn the capability of skill curation for self-evolving agents. We study skill curation in a modular multi-agent framework in a streaming setting, where a frozen agent executor solves tasks with a skill collection (termed SKILLREPO), while a trainable skill curator updates and manages this collection through function calls (Figure 1(a)). We represent skills as Markdown files (Anthropic, 2025b) (Figure 1(b)) managed via file I/O operations similar to an operating system (OS). Our recipe features two core designs. First, we construct each training instance as a group of related tasks. By mimicking test-time streaming settings, it grounds skill curation in long-term utility: skills induced from earlier experiences are evaluated by their ability to improve later related tasks. Second, we design rewards to better attribute environmental feedback to curation decisions, combining task performance with signals for valid function calls, skill quality, and SKILLREPO's compactness. Together, these designs turn delayed and indirect supervision into learning signals for skill curation. 为应对这一挑战，我们提出 SkillOS：一种由经验驱动的 RL 训练配方，用于学习自我演化智能体的技能策划能力。我们在流式场景下的模块化多智能体框架中研究技能策划：冻结的智能体执行器通过一组技能集合（称为 SKILLREPO）来解任务，而可训练的技能策划器通过函数调用更新并管理该集合（图 1(a)）。我们将技能表示为 Markdown 文件（Anthropic，2025b）（图 1(b)），并通过类似操作系统（OS）的文件 I/O 操作进行管理。我们的配方包含两项核心设计。首先，我们将每个训练实例构建为一组相关任务。通过模拟测试时的流式设置，它使技能策划立足于长期效用：由更早经验诱导得到的技能，会根据其对后续相关任务的改进能力进行评估。其次，我们设计奖励，以便更好地将环境反馈归因到策划决策中：在结合任务表现的同时，还加入有效函数调用、技能质量以及 SKILLREPO 紧凑性的信号。两项设计共同把延迟且间接的监督转化为学习信号，用于技能策划。

                                                                                                                                              We evaluate SKILLOS on both multi-turn agentic tasks and single-turn reasoning tasks. Experiments show that SKILLOS consistently outperforms memory-free and strong memory-based methods in both effectiveness and efficiency, with up to +9.8% relative performance improvement and -6.0% fewer interaction steps compared to the strongest baseline (Table 1). Our trained skill curator generalizes well across executors and tasks, improving performance even with the Gemini-2.5-Pro executor. Notably, our 8B curator also outperforms Gemini-2.5-Pro when used directly as the curator. Beyond performance gains, our analyses further show that the learned skill curator leads to more targeted and effective skill utilization, while the skills in SKILLREPO evolve into more richly structured Markdown files that encode higher-level meta-skills over time. Together, we establish SKILLOS as a practical, modular, and experience-driven RL training recipe for building self-evolving agents. 我们在多轮智能体任务和单轮推理任务上评估 SKILLOS。实验表明，SKILLOS 在有效性与效率上均始终优于无记忆方法和强记忆方法，相对性能提升最高可达 +9.8%，且相比最强基线交互步数减少 -6.0%（表 1）。我们训练得到的技能策划器在不同执行器与任务上泛化良好，即使将 Gemini-2.5-Pro 作为执行器也能提升性能。值得注意的是，当策划器被直接用作策划器时，我们的 8B 策划器也优于 Gemini-2.5-Pro。除性能提升外，我们的分析进一步表明：学习到的技能策划器会带来更有针对性且更有效的技能使用，而 SKILLREPO 中的技能会随着时间演化为结构更丰富的 Markdown 文件，从中编码更高层级的元技能。综上，我们将 SKILLOS 作为一种可落地的、模块化的、由经验驱动的 RL 训练配方，用于构建自我演化智能体。

                                                                                                                                              2. Related Work

                                                                                                                                              2. 相关工作

                                                                                                                                              Memory for Self-Evolving Agents. Learning from past experiences as procedural memory (Hu et al., 2025; Huang et al., 2026; Shen et al., 2026; Wei et al., 2025; Wu et al., 2025b; Zhang et al., 2024) is a central mechanism for developing self-evolving agents (Fang et al., 2025a; Gao et al., 2025). The central challenge is to encode interaction histories into reusable and retrievable representations. 面向自演化智能体的记忆。从过往经验中学习并将其作为程序性记忆（Hu et al., 2025; Huang et al., 2026; Shen et al., 2026; Wei et al., 2025; Wu et al., 2025b; Zhang et al., 2024）是构建自演化智能体（Fang et al., 2025a; Gao et al., 2025）的核心机制。其核心挑战在于将交互历史编码为可复用、可检索的表示。

                                                                                                                                              Case-based representations are the most concrete form in this research line: they store experiences in minimally processed formats, allowing past histories to be replayed directly or reused as in-context exemplars, such as raw trajectories (Wu et al., 2025a; Zheng et al., 2024; Zhou et al., 2025) and abstracted query-response pairs (Islam et al., 2024; Zhao et al., 2024). Another line of work abstracts experiences into higher-level knowledge that is editable, auditable, and composable, reducing reliance on long trajectory replay and improving both cross-task generalization and efficiency. Such strategy-based memory typically consists of reusable workflows (Tang et al., 2025; Wang et al., 2025d), distilled insights (Ho et al., 2025; Huang et al., 2025; Ouyang et al., 2026), and recurring patterns (Kim et al., 2025; Yang et al., 2024). Recently, skills (Alzubi et al., 2026; Kuroki et al., 2025; Li et al., 2026a; Liang et al., 2026; Ling et al., 2026; Wang et al., 2025c; Yang et al., 2026; Zhang et al., 2026a) have emerged as a new agent-native form of memory and an orchestrable capability layer, owing to their modularity and ease of customization. Anthropic conceptualizes each skill as a folder containing instructions, scripts, and supporting resources (Anthropic, 2025a), which has become the most widely adopted design in the current community. Our work follows this design philosophy, simplifying the setting for research purposes by representing each skill as a single Markdown file. 基于案例的表示是这一研究方向中最具体的形式：它们以最少处理的格式存储经验，使过去的历史能够被直接重放，或作为上下文示例复用，例如原始轨迹（Wu et al., 2025a; Zheng et al., 2024; Zhou et al., 2025）和抽象的问答对（Islam et al., 2024; Zhao et al., 2024）。另一类工作将经验抽象为更高层次、可编辑、可审计且可组合的知识，从而减少对长轨迹重放的依赖，并提升跨任务泛化能力与效率。这类基于策略的记忆通常由可复用工作流（Tang et al., 2025; Wang et al., 2025d）、提炼出的见解（Ho et al., 2025; Huang et al., 2025; Ouyang et al., 2026）以及反复出现的模式（Kim et al., 2025; Yang et al., 2024）构成。近期，技能（Alzubi et al., 2026; Kuroki et al., 2025; Li et al., 2026a; Liang et al., 2026; Ling et al., 2026; Wang et al., 2025c; Yang et al., 2026; Zhang et al., 2026a）因其模块化和易定制性，已成为一种新的原生于智能体的记忆形式，以及可编排的能力层。Anthropic 将每个技能概念化为一个包含说明、脚本和辅助资源的文件夹（Anthropic, 2025a），这已成为当前社区最广泛采用的设计。我们的工作遵循这一设计理念，为研究目的将每个技能简化为单个 Markdown 文件。

                                                                                                                                              Learning Memory and Skill Curation with RL. Training LLM-based agent systems with memory capabilities using RL has become a growing research direction. One research line targets training for long-context management with predefined operations such as compaction (Wang et al., 2025b; Yu et al., 2026; Zhou et al., 2026). Another interesting area focuses more on memory utilization and management by learning additional memory tool-calls (Yan et al., 2025; Zhang et al., 2025a,b) or training policies for different stages, such as memory retrieval (Zhang et al., 2026b). More recently, RL has been applied at various stages of agent skill development. Specifically, SkillRL (Xia et al., 2026) and D2Skill (Tu et al., 2026) teach smaller models to use skills curated from powerful LLMs in an iterative manner. ARISE (Li et al., 2026b) trains a shared policy operating both as skill retriever and worker, with heuristics for skill management. Recent studies have begun to train agents for memory or skill curation (Wang et al., 2025a; Ye et al., 2026), but their supervision is mostly restricted to local adaptation within short task streams. This favors immediately useful operations such as skill insertion, while offering limited signal for complex management operations, such as revising outdated skills and deleting harmful ones. SKILLOS instead formulates skill curation as a long-horizon, executor-grounded learning problem. We group related tasks into training instances and combine downstream task outcomes with intermediate rewards, turning delayed and indirect feedback into learning signals for skill curation. 使用 RL 学习记忆和技能筛选。利用 RL 训练具备记忆能力的 LLM 智能体系统已成为一个不断增长的研究方向。一条研究线致力于通过预定义操作（如压缩）（Wang et al., 2025b; Yu et al., 2026; Zhou et al., 2026）来训练长上下文管理。另一条有趣的方向则更侧重于记忆利用与管理，通过学习额外的记忆工具调用（Yan et al., 2025; Zhang et al., 2025a,b）或为不同阶段训练策略，例如记忆检索（Zhang et al., 2026b）。近年来，RL 也被应用于智能体技能发展的不同阶段。具体而言，SkillRL（Xia et al., 2026）和 D2Skill（Tu et al., 2026）教会较小模型以迭代方式使用由强大 LLM 筛选出的技能。ARISE（Li et al., 2026b）训练一个共享策略，同时作为技能检索器和执行者，并配合技能管理启发式方法。近期研究已开始训练智能体进行记忆或技能筛选（Wang et al., 2025a; Ye et al., 2026），但其监督大多仅限于短任务流中的局部适应。这更偏向于直接有用的操作，例如插入技能，而对复杂的管理操作，如修订过时技能和删除有害技能，提供的信号有限。相较之下，SKILLOS 将技能筛选表述为一个长时程、以执行器为依据的学习问题。我们将相关任务分组为训练实例，并结合下游任务结果与中间奖励，把延迟且间接的反馈转化为用于技能筛选的学习信号。

                                                                                                                                              3. Methodology

                                                                                                                                              3. 方法论

                                                                                                                                              In this section, we first formalize the problem setting and introduce the multi-agent modular design of SKILLOS. We then detail the RL training recipe designed specifically for training the skill curator. 在本节中，我们首先形式化问题场景，并介绍 SKILLOS 的多智能体模块化设计。随后，我们将详细说明专为训练技能策展器而设计的强化学习训练方案。

                                                                                                                                              3.1. Streaming Skill Curation with Multi-Agent Modular Design

                                                                                                                                              3.1. 流式场景下的多智能体模块化技能策展

                                                                                                                                              We consider a streaming test-time setting (Wu et al., 2024), where an LLM-based agent is deployed to solve a sequence of tasks D={x1,x2,…,xT} that arrive over time. At each time stamp t ,the agent must solve the current task xt before observing future tasks,producing an execution trajectory ξt={o1,a1,…,on,an} ,where o and a denote observations and actions,respectively. This setting naturally captures the challenge of self-evolving agents, where the system must distill useful experience from the trajectories of past interactions to improve performance on future tasks, and become more capable over time. Figure 1(a) presents an overview of the system. 我们考虑一种流式测试时（Wu et al., 2024）设置：将基于LLM的智能体部署以解决一系列随时间到来的任务 D={x1,x2,…,xT} 。在每个时间戳 t ，智能体必须在观察未来任务之前先解决当前任务 xt ，从而生成执行轨迹 ξt={o1,a1,…,on,an} 。其中 o 与 a 分别表示观测与动作。该设置自然地刻画了自进化智能体的挑战：系统需要从过去交互的轨迹中提炼有用经验，以提升未来任务上的表现，并随着时间推移变得更强。图1(a)展示了系统概览。

                                                                                                                                              Skill Repository. We maintain an external skill repository St at time stamp t ,which consists of 技能仓库。我们在时间戳 t 维护一个外部技能仓库 St ，其中包含

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              Figure 2 | SKILLOS training pipeline. Each training step samples a group of related tasks and initializes an empty SKILLREPO. πS is optimized with composite rewards,enabling self-evolution. 图2 | SKILLOS 训练流程。每个训练步骤会采样一组相关任务，并初始化一个空的 SKILLREPO。 πS 通过复合奖励进行优化，从而实现自进化。

                                                                                                                                              Nt reusable skills St={st1,st2,…,stNt} . Following the widely adopted SKILL.md format (Anthropic, 2025b), each skill is represented as a single Markdown file with two components as shown in Figure 1(b): (i) YAML frontmatter, which specifies the skill name and a natural-language description of when the skill should be used, and (ii) Markdown instructions, which describe the executable knowledge, workflows, constraints, and reusable heuristics captured by the skill. Nt 可复用技能 St={st1,st2,…,stNt} 。遵循广泛采用的 SKILL.md 格式（Anthropic, 2025b），每个技能都用一个单独的 Markdown 文件表示，如图1(b)所示由两部分构成：(i) YAML frontmatter，用于指定技能名称以及需要在何时使用的自然语言描述；(ii) Markdown 指令，用于描述技能捕获的可执行知识、工作流、约束以及可复用的启发式规则。

                                                                                                                                              Agent Executor. Given a task xt ,a frozen agent executor πL solves the task conditioning on the current environment observation and relevant skills. Specifically,we retrieve a subset of skills S~t⊆St using BM25 (Robertson and Zaragoza,2009) for each task xt ,and the executor samples actions following a∼πL(⋅∣xt,ot,S~t) . 智能体执行器。给定任务 xt ，一个冻结的智能体执行器 πL 会在当前环境观测与相关技能的条件下解决该任务。具体来说，我们对每个任务 xt 使用 BM25（Robertson and Zaragoza, 2009）检索一部分技能 S~t⊆St ，然后执行器按照 a∼πL(⋅∣xt,ot,S~t) 采样动作。

                                                                                                                                              Skill Curator. After the executor completes task xt ,the skill curator πS observes the trajectory ξt ,the self-judged correctness of the answers/interactions 1ξt ,and a retrieved subset of related skills S~t . It then generates a sequence of structured curation operations ct=(ut1,…,utMt)∼πS(⋅)ξt,1ξt,S~t) ,where each operation utm is one of {insert_skill,update_skill }. Each operation is implemented as a function call (detailed signature in Figure 8) that manipulates the skill repository St . Applying these operations transforms the repository from St to St+1 as St+1=APPLYOPS(St,ct) . The updated repository is then used by the executor on subsequent tasks, forming a closed loop between task execution and experience-driven skill evolution. 技能策展器。当执行器完成任务 xt 后，技能策展器 πS 会观察轨迹 ξt 、对答案/交互的自我评估正确性 1ξt ，以及检索到的相关技能子集 S~t 。随后，它会生成一串结构化的策展操作 ct=(ut1,…,utMt)∼πS(⋅)ξt,1ξt,S~t) ，其中每个操作 utm 都是 {insert_skill,update_skill } 之一。每个操作都通过一次函数调用实现（详见图8中的签名），用于操作技能仓库 St 。应用这些操作后，仓库会从 St 变为 St+1 ，如 St+1=APPLYOPS(St,ct) 所示。更新后的仓库随后会在后续任务上被执行器使用，从而在任务执行与基于经验的技能演化之间形成闭环。

                                                                                                                                              3.2. Learning Skill Curation with RL

                                                                                                                                              3.2. 结合 RL 的技能筛选

                                                                                                                                              We optimize the skill curator πS with RL and keep the agent executor πL frozen. The main challenge is indirect and delayed feedback for curation decisions,which is only revealed through πL ’s performance on future relevant tasks. We address this by constructing grouped training instances (§ 3.2.1) and designing a composite reward (§ 3.2.2) that combines future task outcomes with intermediate signals on operation validity, skill quality, and the conciseness of skills. An overview of the training process is shown in Figure 2. 我们用 RL 优化技能筛选器 πS，并保持智能体执行器 πL 冻结不变。主要挑战在于筛选决策只能通过 πL 在未来相关任务上的表现间接且延迟地反馈。为此，我们构建了分组训练实例（§ 3.2.1），并设计了一个复合奖励（§ 3.2.2），将未来任务结果与关于操作有效性、技能质量以及技能简洁性的中间信号结合起来。训练过程概览见图 2。

                                                                                                                                              3.2.1. Training Instance Construction

                                                                                                                                              3.2.1. 训练实例构建

                                                                                                                                              To provide downstream learning signals for skill curation, we construct each training instance as a group of related tasks that are solved sequentially. Within each group, SKILLREPO is updated by the curator πf after each task,allowing skills derived from earlier experiences to be evaluated by whether they help solve related future tasks. This also differs from prior work that focuses on short-horizon transfer (Wang et al., 2025a; Ye et al., 2026), where our grouped formulation exposes the curator to longer skill-evolution trajectories and provides denser feedback for learning complex curation operations. 为向技能策划提供下游学习信号，我们将每个训练实例构造成一组按顺序求解的相关任务。在每个组内，SKILLREPO 会在每个任务后由策划器 πf 更新，使得由早期经验衍生的技能可通过它们是否有助于解决相关的未来任务来加以评估。这也不同于以短期迁移为重点的先前工作（Wang et al., 2025a; Ye et al., 2026）；在我们的分组形式下，策划器接触到更长的技能演化轨迹，并为学习复杂的策划操作提供更密集的反馈。

                                                                                                                                              Concretely,for each task xi in D={xi}i=1N ,we first annotate each instance with a set of skill-relevant attributes. Formally,for each xi ,we use Gemini-2.5-Pro (Team,2025a) to produce a set of tags: 具体而言，对于 xi 中的每个任务 D={xi}i=1N，我们首先为每个实例标注一组与技能相关的属性。形式上，对于每个 xi，我们使用 Gemini-2.5-Pro (Team,2025a) 生成一组标签：

                                                                                                                                              Zi={zi1,zi2,…,zi|Zi|}

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              where each attribute zi captures a salient aspect of the task xi ,such as topic and common pitfalls. For example, in mathematical reasoning, attributes may include labels such as "algebra" or "Fourier transformation". These attributes serve as proxies for task-relatedness and potential skill dependency. 其中，每个属性 zi 都刻画了任务 xi 的一个显著方面，例如主题和常见陷阱。例如，在数学推理中，属性可以包括“代数”或“傅里叶变换”等标签。这些属性可作为任务相关性和潜在技能依赖性的代理。

                                                                                                                                              Based on the annotated attributes,we then partition D into a collection of M task groups using the similarity of attributes of these data samples: 基于这些已标注的属性，我们再根据这些数据样本属性的相似性，将 D 划分为若干 M 任务组：

                                                                                                                                              D={G1,G2,…,GM},Gm={xm,1,xm,2,…,xm,|Gm|},

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              where all instances within the same group Gm exhibit non-trivial dependency in terms of required skills. Detailed description of data processing and grouping algorithms can be found in Appendix B.2. 其中，同一组 Gm 内的所有实例在所需技能方面都表现出非平凡的依赖关系。数据处理和分组算法的详细说明见附录 B.2。

                                                                                                                                              3.2.2. Training Loop and Policy Optimization

                                                                                                                                              3.2.2. 训练循环与策略优化

                                                                                                                                              We employ Grouped Reward Policy Optimization (GRPO Shao et al. (2024)) for its training stability and sample efficiency. The training loop shown in Algorithm 1 optimizes the skill curator policy πS to maximize a composite reward function over the distribution of generated traces. For a task group G=(x1,…,x|G|) ,the curator produces a sequence of curation decisions c=(c1,…,c|G|) as the executor proceeds through the group. Each training step, the reward combines four signals: 我们采用分组奖励策略优化（GRPO Shao et al. (2024)），因其训练稳定性和样本效率。算法 1 所示的训练循环优化技能策展策略 πS，以最大化生成轨迹分布上的复合奖励函数。对于任务组 G=(x1,…,x|G|)，随着执行器在该组中推进，策展器会生成一系列策展决策 c=(c1,…,c|G|)。每个训练步中，奖励由四个信号组成：

                                                                                                                                              (1)r=r task ⏟ task outcome +λfrfc⏟ function call +λur cnt ⏟ content quality +λcr comp ⏟ compression 

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              Task outcome reward. The first task uses an empty SKILLREPO, before any curator update occurs. We thus define the task outcome reward as the average success over the remaining tasks as r task =1|G|−1∑i=2|G|⁡1(ξi) ,which provides executor-grounded signal on downstream performance achieved by the evolving SKILLREPO from πS . 任务结果奖励。在任何策展器更新发生之前，第一个任务使用空的 SKILLREPO。我们因此将任务结果奖励定义为剩余任务的平均成功率，即 r task =1|G|−1∑i=2|G|⁡1(ξi)，这为不断演化的 SKILLREPO 在 πS 上取得的下游表现提供了以执行器为基础的信号。

                                                                                                                                              Function call reward. The function call reward measures whether the curator produces valid skill operations. For each curation decision ci ,let Valid(ci) be the fraction of generated function calls that are valid and successfully executed. We define the function call reward as rfc=1|G|∑i=1|G|⁡Valid(ci) . 函数调用奖励。函数调用奖励衡量策展器是否生成了有效的技能操作。对于每个策展决策 ci，设 Valid(ci) 为已生成函数调用中有效且成功执行的比例。我们将函数调用奖励定义为 rfc=1|G|∑i=1|G|⁡Valid(ci)。

                                                                                                                                              Compression reward. To discourage verbatim trajectory copying, we reward concise repository updates. Let Si denote the skill repository after applying ci ,and let χi denote the curator input context at position i . We define r comp =1|G|∑i=1|G|⁡(1−|Si||χi|) ,where |Si| and |χi| denote token lengths. This encourages the curator to distill reusable skills rather than store raw trajectories. 压缩奖励。为抑制对轨迹的逐字复制，我们奖励简洁的仓库更新。设 Si 表示应用 ci 后的技能仓库，设 χi 表示位置 i 处的策展器输入上下文。我们定义 r comp =1|G|∑i=1|G|⁡(1−|Si||χi|)，其中 |Si| 和 |χi| 表示 token 长度。这鼓励策展器提炼可复用的技能，而不是存储原始轨迹。

                                                                                                                                              Content quality reward. The content quality reward evaluates whether the curated skills are semantically meaningful and likely to be useful for future tasks. Let Judge(ci) denote the scalar score assigned by an external judge (Qwen3-32B) ci ,we compute the reward as r cnt =1|G|∑i=1|G|⁡Judge(ci) . 内容质量奖励。内容质量奖励评估所策展的技能在语义上是否有意义，以及是否可能对未来任务有用。设 Judge(ci) 表示外部评审器（Qwen3-32B）赋予的标量评分 ci，我们将奖励计算为 r cnt =1|G|∑i=1|G|⁡Judge(ci)。

                                                                                                                                              For each task group G ,we sample N independent rollouts of the entire curation sequence from πS . Within each rollout,the executor produces trajectory ξi using the skill repository Si resulting from previous curations c<i till task position i with the same training task group,so different rollouts evolve different repository histories. The GRPO advantage is computed as: An=rn−1N∑n′=1N⁡rn′ ,where rn is the composite reward (Eq. 1) for the n -th rollout. We optimize πS with a clipped surrogate objective over all curation steps i=1,…,|G| : 对于每个任务组G，我们从πS中采样N个整个整理序列的独立 rollout。在每个 rollout 中，执行器使用由先前整理c<i得到的技能仓库Si，在相同训练任务组下生成至任务位置i的轨迹ξi，因此不同 rollout 会演化出不同的仓库历史。GRPO 优势计算为：An=rn−1N∑n′=1N⁡rn′，其中rn是第n个 rollout 的复合奖励（式 1）。我们对πS在所有整理步骤i=1,…,|G|上采用裁剪的替代目标进行优化：

                                                                                                                                              (2)L=En[min(ρnAn,clip(ρn,1−ϵ,1+ϵ)An)]

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              Algorithm 1 Training Skill Curator with Task Groups using GRPO 算法 1 使用 GRPO 的任务组技能整理器训练

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              for each training step do 对于每个训练步执行

                                                                                                                                                      G=(x1,…,x|G|),S←∅ - Sample a task group and initialize SkillRepo         G=(x1,…,x|G|),S←∅ - 采样一个任务组并初始化 SkillRepo

                                                                                                                                                      for task index i=1,…,|G| do         对于每个任务索引i=1,…,|G|执行

                                                                                                                                                          S~←BM25(xi,S) - Retrieve relevant skills             S~←BM25(xi,S) - 检索相关技能

                                                                                                                                                          ξi←RUNTASK(S~,πL,xi) - Run inference on frozen executor             ξi←RUNTASK(S~,πL,xi) - 在冻结的执行器上运行推理

                                                                                                                                                          ci∼πS(⋅∣ξi,S~) ⊳ Sample a rollout from skill curator             ci∼πS(⋅∣ξi,S~) ⊳ 从技能整理器采样一个 rollout

                                                                                                                                                          S←ApplyOPs(S,ci) - Apply insert/update/delete             S←ApplyOPs(S,ci) - 执行插入/更新/删除

                                                                                                                                                      end for         end for

                                                                                                                                                      r←CALCULATEREWARD(ξ,c)

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                                      Update πS ⊳ Update skill curator using GRPO         更新πS ⊳ 使用 GRPO 更新技能整理器

                                                                                                                                              end for end for

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              where ρn=πS(cn∣χ)/πθ old (cn∣χ) is the importance ratio. The advantage An is assigned uniformly to all tokens in cn ,and we discard the KL term in GRPO to encourage policy exploration. 其中ρn=πS(cn∣χ)/πθ old (cn∣χ)为重要性比率。优势An均匀分配给cn中的所有 token，并且我们在 GRPO 中舍弃 KL 项以鼓励策略探索。

                                                                                                                                              4. Experiments

                                                                                                                                              4. 实验

                                                                                                                                              We conduct experiments on both multi-turn agentic tasks and single-turn reasoning tasks, in line with prior work (Wei et al., 2025; Xia et al., 2026; Ye et al., 2026). We additionally show that the trained skill curator transfers across agent executors and task domains, highlighting its flexibility and generalizability. 我们在多轮交互的智能体任务和单轮推理任务上开展实验，遵循既有工作（Wei 等，2025；Xia 等，2026；Ye 等，2026）。此外，我们表明训练后的技能策展器能够在不同智能体执行器与任务领域之间迁移，体现出其灵活性与泛化能力。

                                                                                                                                              4.1. Setup

                                                                                                                                              4.1. 设置

                                                                                                                                              We briefly discuss the experiment setup throughout this paper. Full description of datasets, implementations, baselines, and evaluations can be found in Appendix B. 在本文中，我们将简要讨论实验设置。数据集、实现方式、基线和评估的完整描述请见附录 B。

                                                                                                                                              Dataset. For agentic tasks, we conduct experiments on ALFWorld (Shridhar et al., 2021) and WebShop (Yao et al., 2022). ALFWorld is a text-based interactive environment aligned with the ALFRED embodied AI benchmark, where agents must complete household tasks through textual navigation and object manipulation. WebShop simulates an online shopping environment in which agents navigate a realistic web interface to identify and purchase products that satisfy user-specified requirements. For each benchmark,we train SKILLOS on its training split where Zi is the default task type annotations, and evaluate on the corresponding test set. In addition to agentic tasks, we also benchmark for single-turn reasoning tasks, including AIME24, AIME25, and GPQA-Diamond (Rein et al., 2024). Training data are constructed from DeepMath-103k (He et al., 2026a), where we randomly sample a subset of 33,000 data points. 数据集。对于具身智能体任务，我们在 ALFWorld（Shridhar 等，2021）和 WebShop（Yao 等，2022）上进行实验。ALFWorld 是一个与 ALFRED 具身 AI 基准相匹配的基于文本的交互环境，其中智能体必须通过文本导航与物体操作完成家务任务。WebShop 模拟了一个在线购物环境：智能体在真实的网页界面中进行浏览，以识别并购买满足用户指定要求的商品。对每个基准，我们在其训练划分上训练 SKILLOS，其中 Zi 为默认任务类型标注，并在对应的测试集上进行评估。除具身智能体任务外，我们还对单轮推理任务进行基准测试，包括 AIME24、AIME25 和 GPQA-Diamond（Rein 等，2024）。训练数据由 DeepMath-103k（He 等，2026a）构建，我们将随机抽取 33,000 条数据的子集。

                                                                                                                                              Evaluation Configurations. We evaluate all methods across two dimensions, effectiveness and efficiency. For effectiveness, we measure the success rate (SR) and accuracy for agentic tasks and reasoning tasks, respectively. For efficiency, we compute the number of execution steps per agentic task and the number of tokens per reasoning problem, respectively. We compare SKILLOS with three categories of baselines: (i) a memory-free agent (No Memory); (ii) existing memory-based methods, including 评估配置。我们从两个维度评估所有方法：有效性与效率。就有效性而言，我们分别衡量具身智能体任务与推理任务的成功率（SR）和准确性。就效率而言，我们分别计算每个具身智能体任务的执行步数，以及每道推理问题的 token 数。我们将 SKILLOS 与三类基线进行比较：（i）无记忆智能体（No Memory）；（ii）现有基于记忆的方法，包括

                                                                                                                                              Table 1 | Experiment results on ALFWorld benchmark. Success rate (SR ↑ ) and the number of steps (Steps ↓ ) are reported on 6 subsets with 3 different frozen executors. 表 1｜ALFWorld 基准上的实验结果。成功率（SR ↑ ）与步数（Steps ↓ ）在 3 个不同冻结执行器的 6 个子集上报告。

                                                                                                                                              MethodsCurator ${\pi }_{\mathcal{S}}$Pick (35)Look (13)Clean (27)Heat (16)Cool (25)Pick2 (24)Avg. SR (140)Steps
                                                                                                                                              Executor ${\pi }_{\mathcal{L}}$ : Qwen3-8B
                                                                                                                                              No MemoryNone78.146.233.337.5 10.829.3 6.147.247.921.1
                                                                                                                                              ReasoningBank8 Qwen3-8B83.848.749.439.641.3 8.554.255.720.1
                                                                                                                                              MemP* Qwen3-8B80.0 5.743.6424.7 4.333.3 3.638.748.649.7 0.721.0
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-baseQ Quen3-8B79.0 8.74 $\bar{1}$ . ${\bar{0}}_{4.4}$45.737.5 9.538.7 4.055.653.1 2.520.4
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-geminiGemini-2.5-Pro77.1 6.053.837.0 6.437.5 9.536.050.0 6.750.7 3.620.8
                                                                                                                                              SkillosQ Qwen3-8B85.756.454.343.846.7 2.362.5 6.461.2 4.618.9
                                                                                                                                              Executor ${\pi }_{\mathcal{L}}$ : Qwen3-32B
                                                                                                                                              No MemoryNone80.0 2.969.2 0.045.6737.5 16.5${42.7}_{6.1}$43.154.5 2.520.3
                                                                                                                                              ReasoningBankQuen3-8B86.7 3.071.850.6 6345.8${52.0}_{8.9}$51.461.4 2.518.7
                                                                                                                                              MemP\$ Qwen3-8B80.0 2.976.9 0.044.437.5 10.8${42.7}_{2.3}$47.2${55.7}_{3.7}$20.0
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-baseQuen3-8B82.969.2 11.848. ${\overline{1}}_{2.1}$50.0.7${48.0}_{14.4}$52.8${59.8}_{3.0}$19.2
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-gemini& Gemini-2.5-Pro97.1 3.076.9${55.6}_{6.0}$43.8${40.0}_{5.7}$54.2 4.9${63.6}_{4.2}$18.1
                                                                                                                                              SkillOS6 Qwen3-8B91.476.9${59.3}_{8.6}$56.3${\mathbf{{57.3}}}_{10.1}$62.5 4.2${68.6}_{5.7}$17.3
                                                                                                                                              Executor ${\pi }_{f}$ : Gemini-2.5-pro
                                                                                                                                              No MemoryNone90.5 3.266.7${48.1}_{10.2}$39.6 17.16868.1${76.4}_{2.0}$17.7
                                                                                                                                              ReasoningBankQ Wen3-8B91.461.5 4.163.039.6 17.170.7 3.276.4${71.4}_{2.9}$16.0
                                                                                                                                              MemPOwen3-8B95.2 2.174.4 6.861.756.3 12.476.0 6.268.174.3 3.415.2
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-baseBowen3-8B91.4 1.669.256.854.27 ${\overline{2.0}}_{4.0}$66.770.7 3.016.3
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-geminiGemini-2.5-Pro94.369.2 0.077.875.0 16.580.0 12.266.7 2.479.3 2.614.9
                                                                                                                                              SkillOS4 Qwen3-8B95.2 2.971.874.172.9 10.177.3 6.177.880.2 3.114.8
                                                                                                                                              方法策展器 ${\pi }_{\mathcal{S}}$拾取 (35)查看 (13)清洁 (27)加热 (16)冷却 (25)双重拾取 (24)平均成功率 (140)步骤
                                                                                                                                              执行器 ${\pi }_{\mathcal{L}}$ : Qwen3-8B
                                                                                                                                              无记忆无78.146.233.337.5 10.829.3 6.147.247.921.1
                                                                                                                                              ReasoningBank8 Qwen3-8B83.848.749.439.641.3 8.554.255.720.1
                                                                                                                                              MemP* Qwen3-8B80.0 5.743.6424.7 4.333.3 3.638.748.649.7 0.721.0
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-baseQ Quen3-8B79.0 8.74 $\bar{1}$ . ${\bar{0}}_{4.4}$45.737.5 9.538.7 4.055.653.1 2.520.4
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-geminiGemini-2.5-Pro77.1 6.053.837.0 6.437.5 9.536.050.0 6.750.7 3.620.8
                                                                                                                                              SkillosQ Qwen3-8B85.756.454.343.846.7 2.362.5 6.461.2 4.618.9
                                                                                                                                              执行器 ${\pi }_{\mathcal{L}}$ : Qwen3-32B
                                                                                                                                              无记忆无80.0 2.969.2 0.045.6737.5 16.5${42.7}_{6.1}$43.154.5 2.520.3
                                                                                                                                              ReasoningBankQuen3-8B86.7 3.071.850.6 6345.8${52.0}_{8.9}$51.461.4 2.518.7
                                                                                                                                              MemP\$ Qwen3-8B80.0 2.976.9 0.044.437.5 10.8${42.7}_{2.3}$47.2${55.7}_{3.7}$20.0
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-baseQuen3-8B82.969.2 11.848. ${\overline{1}}_{2.1}$50.0.7${48.0}_{14.4}$52.8${59.8}_{3.0}$19.2
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-gemini& Gemini-2.5-Pro97.1 3.076.9${55.6}_{6.0}$43.8${40.0}_{5.7}$54.2 4.9${63.6}_{4.2}$18.1
                                                                                                                                              SkillOS6 Qwen3-8B91.476.9${59.3}_{8.6}$56.3${\mathbf{{57.3}}}_{10.1}$62.5 4.2${68.6}_{5.7}$17.3
                                                                                                                                              执行器 ${\pi }_{f}$ : Gemini-2.5-pro
                                                                                                                                              无记忆无90.5 3.266.7${48.1}_{10.2}$39.6 17.16868.1${76.4}_{2.0}$17.7
                                                                                                                                              ReasoningBankQ Wen3-8B91.461.5 4.163.039.6 17.170.7 3.276.4${71.4}_{2.9}$16.0
                                                                                                                                              MemPOwen3-8B95.2 2.174.4 6.861.756.3 12.476.0 6.268.174.3 3.415.2
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-baseBowen3-8B91.4 1.669.256.854.27 ${\overline{2.0}}_{4.0}$66.770.7 3.016.3
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-geminiGemini-2.5-Pro94.369.2 0.077.875.0 16.580.0 12.266.7 2.479.3 2.614.9
                                                                                                                                              SkillOS4 Qwen3-8B95.2 2.971.874.172.9 10.177.3 6.177.880.2 3.114.8

                                                                                                                                              ReasoningBank (Ouyang et al., 2026), which distills reusable insights from past experiences, and MemP (Fang et al., 2025b), which induces procedural memory with advanced memory-management strategies; and (iii) internal variants of our framework, including SKILLOS-base, which uses the initial skill curator without RL training, and SKILLOS-gemini, which uses Gemini-2.5-Pro to directly perform skill curation instead of learning the curator with RL. All prompts used can be found in Appendix A. ReasoningBank（Ouyang et al., 2026）从以往经验中提炼可复用的洞见，以及 MemP（Fang et al., 2025b），通过先进的记忆管理策略诱导程序性记忆；以及（iii）我们框架的内部变体，包括 SKILLOS-base：使用最初的技能策展器且不进行 RL 训练；SKILLOS-gemini：使用 Gemini-2.5-Pro 直接执行技能策展，而不是用 RL 学习策展器。所有使用的提示词可在附录 A 中找到。

                                                                                                                                              Implementation Details. We use Qwen3-8B (Team,2025b) as the base model for πS . The frozen executor is also instantiated with Qwen3-8B during training. We train our model using GRPO with a learning rate 1×10−6 ,batch size 32,and group size 8 . Training is conducted on 16 H100 GPUs using the ver1 framework (Sheng et al., 2024). The full training process takes approximately 3 days for ALFWorld, 2.5 days for reasoning tasks, and 5 days for WebShop. For testing, we additionally include Qwen3-32B, Gemini-2.5-Pro (Team, 2025a), and Gemini-3.1-Flash-Lite (Appendix C.1) as executors to evaluate the generalization of SKILLOS under different executor scales and architectures. Task outcome signal 1ξt is obtained via LLM-as-a-judge with the frozen agent executor (prompt shown in Appendix A). We use ReAct (Yao et al., 2023) for agent execution and CoT (Wei et al., 2022a) for reasoning tasks. For the reward function,we set λf=1.0,λu=0.1 ,and λc=0.05 . We report averaged performance and standard deviation over 3 runs. 实现细节。我们以 πS 为基础模型，使用 Qwen3-8B（Team,2025b）。训练期间，冻结的执行器也用 Qwen3-8B 实例化。我们使用 GRPO 训练模型，学习率为 1×10−6，批大小为 32，组大小为 8。训练在 16 张 H100 GPU 上进行，采用 ver1 框架（Sheng et al., 2024）。完整训练过程：在 ALFWorld 上约 3 天，在推理任务上 2.5 天，在 WebShop 上 5 天。测试时，我们还额外加入 Qwen3-32B、Gemini-2.5-Pro（Team, 2025a）和 Gemini-3.1-Flash-Lite（附录 C.1）作为执行器，以评估在不同执行器规模与架构下 SKILLOS 的泛化能力。任务结果信号 1ξt 通过使用冻结的智能体执行器的 LLM-as-a-judge 获得（提示词见附录 A）。智能体执行使用 ReAct（Yao et al., 2023），推理任务使用 CoT（Wei et al., 2022a）。奖励函数中，我们设定 λf=1.0,λu=0.1 和 λc=0.05。我们在 3 次运行中报告平均性能与标准差。

                                                                                                                                              4.2. Main Results

                                                                                                                                              4.2. 主要结果

                                                                                                                                              Tables 1 and 2 summarize the results for different benchmarks with Qwen3-8B as the skill curator on various agent executors. Based on the results, we have the following observations. 表 1 和表 2 汇总了在不同基准上、由 Qwen3-8B 作为技能策划器并应用于各类智能体执行器的结果。基于这些结果，我们得到如下观察。

                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS achieves strong performance gains across benchmarks. Across all three benchmarks, as action ordering, exploration strategies, recovery behaviors, and environment-specific constraints, which can be repeatedly composed and refined across task streams. Reasoning tasks also benefit from skill curation, but their reusable knowledge often appears at a more abstract level, such as decomposition heuristics, constraint formulation, or verification patterns, rather than as directly reusable action procedures. As a result, SKILLOS still improves reasoning performance, while the gains are typically smaller than those observed on agentic benchmarks. We provide a case study demonstrating skills curated for different tasks in Figure 17. SKILLOS 在各个基准上都取得了显著的性能提升。在所有三个基准中，行动排序、探索策略、恢复行为以及面向环境的特定约束都能被反复地在任务流程中进行组合与精炼。推理任务同样受益于技能策划，但其可复用知识往往呈现为更抽象的层面，例如分解启发式、约束表述或验证模式，而不是以可直接复用的行动流程形式出现。因此，SKILLOS 仍能提升推理性能，但通常小于在智能体基准上观察到的增幅。我们在图 17 中给出了一个案例研究，展示了为不同任务策划的技能。

                                                                                                                                              Table 2 | Experiment results on WebShop and single-turn reasoning tasks for 3 different frozen executors. For WebShop,the averaged score,success rate (SR ↑ ),and the number of steps (Steps ↓ ) are reported. For reasoning tasks, accuracy (Acc. ↑) is reported on three datasets. 表 2 | 3 个不同冻结执行器在 WebShop 与单轮推理任务上的实验结果。对于 WebShop，报告平均分、成功率（SR ↑）以及步骤数（Steps ↓）。对于推理任务，在三个数据集上报告准确率（Acc. ↑）。

                                                                                                                                              ${\pi }_{\mathcal{S}}$                                                                                                                                              		WebShop推理
                                                                                                                                              得分SR步数AIME24AIME25GPQA平均准确率
                                                                                                                                              执行器 ${\pi }_{\mathcal{L}}$ : Qwen3-8B

                                                                                                                                              ReasoningBank& Qwen3-8B35.411.4 0.920.575.473.2 10.860.369.6
                                                                                                                                              MemPQWen3-8B35.7 0.912.0 0.521.375.671.160.6 4.069.1 4.0

                                                                                                                                              SKILLOSA Qwen3-8B40.616.5 0.719.480.0 3.376.7 5.864.6${\mathbf{{73.8}}}_{1.8}$
                                                                                                                                              执行器 ${\pi }_{\mathcal{L}}$ : Qwen3-32B
                                                                                                                                              无记忆无41.5 0.512.2 0.317.081.472.2 3.868.474.0
                                                                                                                                              ReasoningBankQWen3-32B40.411.2 1.117.981.1 9.675.666.974.9
                                                                                                                                              MemPQwen3-32B30.7 0.710.117.482.2 5.176.7 0.066.5 2.375.1
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-baseOwen3-8B43.412.3 1.016.880.075.667.7 1.574.7 3.3
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-gemini& Gemini-2.5-pro45.2 1.013.2 1.116.677.874.466.2 0.673.2
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOSA Qwen3-8B49.216.5 0.615.985.681.172.479.7 1.6
                                                                                                                                              执行器 ${\pi }_{\mathcal{L}}$ : Gemini-2.5-pro
                                                                                                                                              无记忆无48.638.419.585.680.0 6.779.9 1.581.8
                                                                                                                                              ReasoningBankGemini-2.5-pro50.8 1.540.219.285.684.480.483.5
                                                                                                                                              MemPGemini-2.5-pro51.3 1.239.819.483.376.7 5.881.880.6
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-baseOwen3-8B52.8 1.039.619.087.883.382.8${84.6}_{1.8}$
                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS-gemini& Gemini-2.5-pro54.741.0 1.217.890.0 5.185.680.7 5.5${85.4}_{3.5}$
                                                                                                                                              SkillosA Qwen3-8B56.0 0.741.3 0.818.392.2 2.486.7 3.586.8${\mathbf{{88.6}}}_{1.5}$

                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS consistently outperforms both memory-free and memory-based baselines, showing that the gains come from learning to manage and evolve skills rather than from maintaining a static collection. On ALFWorld, SKILLOS improves the average success rate from 55.7 to 61.2 over the strongest baseline ReasoningBank with Qwen3-8B as the executor; similar trends hold on WebShop and reasoning tasks. Strikingly, our RL-trained 8B curator even surpasses SKILLOS-gemini, despite the latter using a far stronger frontier model as the curator, demonstrating that targeted training of a small curator can outweigh raw model scale. The benefits brought by RL training are also compounded with executor capacity, yielding +9.5 absolute improvement with Gemini-2.5-Pro versus +7.9 with Qwen3-8B for ALFworld, compared with SKILLOS-base. SKILLOS持续优于无记忆与有记忆两类基线，说明性能提升来自学习管理并进化技能，而非维持一个静态技能集合。在ALFWorld上，SKILLOS相较于以Qwen3-8B作执行器的最强基线ReasoningBank，平均成功率从55.7提升到61.2；在WebShop与推理任务上也呈现相似趋势。令人惊讶的是，经过RL训练的8B策划器甚至超过了SKILLOS-gemini，尽管后者使用了更强大的前沿模型作为策划器，这表明对小型策划器的定向训练可以胜过单纯的模型规模。RL训练带来的收益还会随着执行器能力叠加：在ALFworld上，相比SKILLOS-base，Gemini-2.5-Pro带来+9.5的绝对提升，而Qwen3-8B带来+7.9。

                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS is more efficient, requiring fewer interactions and lower execution cost. The gains of SKILLOS are accompanied by better efficiency rather than longer trajectories. On ALFWorld, SKILLOS reduces the average interaction steps by 2.2, 3.0, and 3.1 compared with "no memory" setting with 3 executors, consistently outperforming all memory-based baselines. This trend extends to WebShop, where SKILLOS secures higher success rates with fewer environment interactions. These results indicate that the learned skill manager enables the executor to identify procedural shortcuts and bypass redundant exploration. Rather than relying on additional trial-and-error, SKILLOS improves performance by distilling experience into direct, actionable expertise that simplifies task execution. SKILLOS更高效，需要更少的交互并降低执行成本。SKILLOS的收益伴随更好的效率，而不是更长的轨迹。在ALFWorld上，SKILLOS相较于配有3个执行器的“无记忆”设置，平均交互步数减少2.2、3.0和3.1，并始终优于所有基于记忆的基线。该趋势延伸到WebShop：SKILLOS以更少的环境交互实现更高的成功率。这些结果表明，学到的技能管理器使执行器能够识别程序捷径并跳过冗余探索。SKILLOS并非依赖额外的反复试错，而是通过将经验蒸馏成直接可执行的专业知识来提升任务执行效果，从而简化流程。

                                                                                                                                              The gains differ between agentic and reasoning tasks, reflecting different forms of reusable skills. A notable trend is that the gains of SKILLOS are generally larger on multi-turn agentic benchmarks than on single-turn reasoning tasks. We hypothesize that this difference arises from how reusable skills manifest across task types. Agentic tasks naturally expose procedural regularities, such 在代理式任务与推理任务之间，收益有所不同，反映了可复用技能的不同表现形式。一个显著趋势是，SKILLOS在多轮代理式基准上的收益通常大于在单轮推理任务上的收益。我们推测这种差异源于可复用技能在不同任务类型中的显现方式。代理式任务天然暴露出程序上的规律性，这

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              Figure 3 | Cross-task generalization results of SKILLOS with (a) Qwen3-8B, (b) Qwen3-32B, and (c) Gemini-2.5-Pro as frozen executors. We plot relative improvement with baselines from least to most. 图3 | SKILLOS在跨任务上的泛化结果：将(a) Qwen3-8B、(b) Qwen3-32B和(c) Gemini-2.5-Pro作为冻结的执行器。我们将相对提升按从最小到最大的基线进行绘制。

                                                                                                                                              4.3. Generalization of SKILLOS

                                                                                                                                              4.3. SKILLOS 的泛化

                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS is transferable and remains effective for different agent executors. During training, we use Qwen3-8B as the executor. To test whether SKILLOS brings improvement for executors that are not seen in training, we pair the trained skill curator with different executors. As shown in Table 1 and 2, SKILLOS consistently improves a wide range of frozen executors across benchmarks, from open-source models (Qwen3-8B, Qwen3-32B) to frontier models (Gemini-2.5-Pro). On ALFWorld, it lifts the average success rate of Qwen3-8B from 47.9 to 61.2 and Gemini-2.5-Pro from 66.4 to 80.2, demonstrating compatibility with executors of varying capacity. Notably, using Gemini-2.5-Pro directly as the curator (SKILLOS-gemini) underperforms our trained curator, especially when paired with the smaller Qwen3-8B executor. This highlights a curator-executor mismatch: stronger reasoning ability alone does not guarantee effective skill curation, as frontier-generated skills may be misaligned with the executor's capacity or usage patterns. By contrast, SKILLOS learns executor-grounded curation behaviors through RL, producing skills that better match the downstream agent. SKILLOS 具有可迁移性，并且对不同的智能体执行器仍然有效。训练期间，我们使用 Qwen3-8B 作为执行器。为测试 SKILLOS 是否能给训练中未见过的执行器带来提升，我们将训练好的技能策展器与不同执行器配对。如表1和表2所示，SKILLOS 能持续提升一系列冻结执行器在各项基准上的表现，从开源模型（Qwen3-8B、Qwen3-32B）到前沿模型（Gemini-2.5-Pro）均然如此。在 ALFWorld 上，它将 Qwen3-8B 的平均成功率从 47.9 提升到 61.2，并将 Gemini-2.5-Pro 从 66.4 提升到 80.2，表明其与不同能力的执行器兼容。值得注意的是，直接使用 Gemini-2.5-Pro 作为策展器（SKILLOS-gemini）的表现不如我们训练得到的策展器，尤其是在与较小的 Qwen3-8B 执行器配对时。这凸显了策展器与执行器的不匹配：更强的推理能力本身并不能保证有效的技能策展，因为前沿模型生成的技能可能与执行器的能力或使用模式不一致。相比之下，SKILLOS 通过 RL 学习到以执行器为基础的策展行为，生成了更符合下游智能体的技能。

                                                                                                                                              SKILLOS delivers consistent performance improvement when generalized to different task domains. Figure 3 shows that the skill curator learned by SKILLOS transfers well across different tasks. While training and testing on the same task often gives the strongest gain, most off-diagonal entries still bring performance improvement over baselines, indicating that SKILLOS captures reusable skills beyond task-specific heuristics. Specifically,skill curator πs learned from reasoning tasks transfer particularly well to the two agentic tasks, likely because they contain more abstract and high-level strategies, such as decomposition, verification, and adaptive planning, which are broadly useful across settings. In contrast, skills learned from WebShop or ALFWorld are more tied to environment-specific knowledge, making them less transferable across tasks. 当泛化到不同任务领域时，SKILLOS 也能带来稳定的性能提升。图3显示，SKILLOS 学到的技能策展器在不同任务间具有良好的迁移性。虽然在同一任务上训练和测试通常能获得最强增益，但大多数非对角线结果仍然相比基线带来性能提升，说明 SKILLOS 捕捉到的是可复用技能，而非任务特定启发式。具体而言，从推理任务中学到的技能策展器πs对两类智能体任务的迁移效果尤佳，这很可能是因为它们包含更抽象、更高层的策略，例如分解、验证和自适应规划，这些策略在不同场景中都很有用。相比之下，从 WebShop 或 ALFWorld 中学到的技能更依赖环境特定知识，因此跨任务迁移性较弱。

                                                                                                                                              5. Analysis

                                                                                                                                              5. 分析

                                                                                                                                              Beyond performance, we analyze why SKILLOS works, focusing on design choices, evolution of curator's behaviors and contents in SKILLREPO, and the role of curated skills in task success. Additional analyses are included in Appendix C. 超越性能，我们分析为什么 SKILLOS 有效，重点关注设计选择、SKILLREPO 中策展人（curator）行为与内容的演化，以及被策展技能在任务成功中的作用。附加分析见附录 C。

                                                                                                                                              Ablation Studies. We ablate two components of SKILLOS: (i) auxiliary rewards in Eq. 1, and (ii) grouped task streams in § 3.2.1. Experiments are conducted on ALFWorld, with Qwen3-8B used as both the curator and executor. As shown in Table 3, removing either reward component hurts performance. Without the content-quality reward, the success rate drops from 61.2 to 58.6, showing the importance of intermediate supervision for guiding skill updates in a pipelined system. Removing the compression reward causes a smaller but consistent drop, suggesting that concise repositories are easier for the executor to use. The most significant degradation comes from using random task sequences (w/o grouping), which lowers the success rate to 57.3. This highlights the importance of training on grouped task streams, in which curation decisions are learned from their downstream impact on related future tasks. 消融研究。我们对 SKILLOS 的两个组成部分进行消融：（i）式 1 中的辅助奖励，以及（ii）§ 3.2.1 中的分组任务流。实验在 ALFWorld 上进行，使用 Qwen3-8B 作为策展人和执行器。正如表 3 所示，移除任一奖励成分都会降低性能。没有内容质量奖励时，成功率从 61.2 降至 58.6，表明在流水线式系统中，中间监督对引导技能更新至关重要。移除压缩奖励会带来幅度较小但稳定的下降，说明更精炼的资源库更容易被执行器使用。最显著的退化来自使用随机任务序列（不分组），这将成功率降至 57.3。这强调了在分组任务流上进行训练的重要性：策展决策通过其对相关未来任务的下游影响来被学习。

                                                                                                                                              Table 3 | Ablation results of reward design on the ALFWorld dataset. 表 3 | ALFWorld 数据集上奖励设计的消融结果。

                                                                                                                                              MethodsAvg. SRSteps
                                                                                                                                              SkillOS-GRPO61.218.9
                                                                                                                                              w/o ${r}^{\text{ cnt }}$58.620.1
                                                                                                                                              w/o ${r}^{\text{ comp }}$60.019.3
                                                                                                                                              w/o grouping57.320.6
                                                                                                                                              方法平均 SR步骤
                                                                                                                                              SkillOS-GRPO61.218.9
                                                                                                                                              不含 ${r}^{\text{ cnt }}$58.620.1
                                                                                                                                              不含 ${r}^{\text{ comp }}$60.019.3
                                                                                                                                              不分组57.320.6

                                                                                                                                              Behaviors of Skill Curator. To better understand how the behavior of the skill curator evolves during training, we analyze the distribution of its three skill operations from rollouts at different training steps: insert_skill , update_skill , and delete_skill . Figure 4 plots the proportion of each operation. At the beginning of training, insert overwhelmingly dominates, indicating that the model is primarily focused on populating the skill repository with new knowledge distilled from experience. As training progresses, however, update becomes increasingly frequent, while insert steadily declines. This suggests that the skill curator gradually moves from plain expansion of skills to refining existing skills. Meanwhile, delete remains a relatively small fraction throughout training with a slightly growing trend, showing the effectiveness of rewarding conciseness of SKILLREPO. Instead, the dominant form of adaptation is to revise and consolidate previously acquired skills. 技能策展人行为。为更好理解技能策展人在训练过程中行为如何演化，我们分析其在不同时训练步长的回合中三种技能操作的分布：insert_skill、update_skill 和 delete_skill。图4展示了各操作所占比例。训练初期，insert 几乎占据主导，表明模型主要专注于从经验中蒸馏新知识，以填充技能库。随着训练推进，update 的频率逐渐升高，而 insert 稳步下降。这说明技能策展人正从单纯扩充技能逐步转向对现有技能进行精炼。与此同时，delete 在整个训练过程中始终只占较小比例，但略有增长，表明奖励 SKILLREPO 的简洁性是有效的。相较之下，占主导的适应方式在于修订并整合先前习得的技能。

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              Figure 4 | Behaviors of the skill curator w.r.t. skill operations during training. 图4 | 训练期间技能策展人相对于技能操作的行为。

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              Figure 5 | Evolution dynamics of the curated skills under RL training. 图5 | 在 RL 训练下被策划技能的演化动力学。

                                                                                                                                              Skill Evolution Dynamics. Beyond aggregate performance, we examine how the skill repository evolves during RL training. We focus on two emergent phenomena: (i) new Markdown sections within individual skills, and (ii) higher-level meta-skills that capture reusable principles across tasks. Figure 5(a) shows that early in training, the curator tends to introduce generic sections such as additional guidance, tips, or recommendations, which often make skills more verbose without substantially improving their operational value. As training progresses, these additions shift toward more actionable structures, such as failure-handling logic and conditional branches that specify when to deviate from the default workflow. This suggests that RL gradually steers the curator from superficial enrichment toward execution-oriented skill refinement. Figure 5(b) further shows that evolution occurs not only within individual skills, but also in the global organization of the repository. Early repositories are dominated by narrow, task-specific skills, whereas later repositories contain a more diverse set of meta-strategy skills covering verification, fallback planning, system search, and strategy adjustment. This indicates that the learned curator does not merely accumulate skills, but progressively expands the repository's strategic space, shifting it from isolated task-local procedures toward more compositional cross-task control knowledge. 技能演化动力学。除了总体性能，我们还考察 RL 训练期间技能库如何演化。我们关注两种涌现现象：（i）各个技能内部新增的 Markdown 小节，以及（ii）跨任务可复用原则所刻画的更高层元技能。图5(a)显示，训练早期，策展人倾向于引入更多通用部分，如额外指导、技巧或建议，这些内容往往让技能更冗长，却并未显著提升其操作价值。随着训练进展，这些新增内容转向更可执行的结构，例如故障处理逻辑和条件分支，用于明确何时偏离默认流程。这表明 RL 正在逐步引导策展人从表面式丰富走向以执行为导向的技能精炼。图5(b)进一步表明，演化不仅发生在单个技能内部，也发生在技能库的全局组织方式上。早期的技能库以较窄的、任务特定的技能为主；而后期的技能库则包含更多元的元策略技能，覆盖验证、回退规划、系统搜索以及策略调整。这说明所学到的策展人并不仅仅是在累积技能，而是逐步扩展技能库的策略空间：将其从孤立的任务本地流程，转向更具组合性的跨任务控制知识。

                                                                                                                                              Attribution of Skill Usage. To better understand whether the gains of SKILLOS come from the evolved skills, we analyze how skills are used during evaluation. We consider 4 complementary metrics: (i) skill usage rate, the fraction of examples where the agent invokes at least one skill; (ii) successful skill usage rate, the success rate among examples that use skills; (iii) skill coverage, the fraction of the skill collection that are actually used; and (iv) the average number of skills used per example, which measures the degree of skill reliance. Figure 6 reports results on ALF-World. Compared with the baseline, SKILLOS invokes skills on all evaluation examples and achieves a higher success rate, indicating that the evolved skills contribute directly to task solving. Also, a larger fraction of the skill curated by SKILLOS is used, showing that RL training improves the overall utility of the curated SKILLREPO. Meanwhile, SKILLOS uses fewer skills per example, suggesting that gains come from more precise skill selection rather than more skill context. 技能使用归因。为更好理解 SKILLOS 的收益是否来自演化后的技能，我们分析评估过程中技能如何被使用。我们考虑 4 个互补指标：（i）技能使用率：代理在至少调用一个技能的样本中所占比例；（ii）成功技能使用率：在使用技能的样本中达到成功的比例；（iii）技能覆盖率：实际被使用的技能集合所占比例；以及（iv）每个样本平均使用的技能数，用于衡量对技能的依赖程度。图6给出了在 ALF-World 上的结果。与基线相比，SKILLOS 在所有评估样本中都会调用技能，并取得更高的成功率，表明演化后的技能直接促进了任务求解。此外，SKILLOS 策划出的技能中被使用的比例更高，说明 RL 训练提升了所策划 SKILLREPO 的整体效用。与此同时，SKILLOS 每个样本使用的技能更少，表明收益来自更精确的技能选择，而不是更多的技能上下文。

                                                                                                                                               

                                                                                                                                              Figure 6 | Comparison of skill utilization statistics on ALFWorld. 图6 | ALFWorld 上技能利用统计的对比。

                                                                                                                                              6. Conclusion

                                                                                                                                              6. 结论

                                                                                                                                              We presented SkillOS, an RL training recipe for learning skill curation in self-evolving agents. By decoupling the skill curator from the agent executor, SKILLOS enables modular skill curation without retraining the underlying executor. Through grouped task streams and executor-grounded rewards, SKILLOS optimizes curation decisions by their downstream impact on future tasks. Across diverse benchmarks and LLM backbones, SKILLOS consistently improves both performance and efficiency. Further analyses show that trained skill curation can outperform frontier models' zero-shot curation ability and generalize across settings, highlighting modular, trained skill curation as a practical path toward agents that self-evolve from experience. 我们提出了 SkillOS，一种用于学习自进化智能体中技能筛选的 RL 训练方案。通过将技能筛选器与智能体执行器解耦，SkillOS 实现了无需重新训练底层执行器的模块化技能筛选。借助分组任务流和基于执行器的奖励，SkillOS 根据其对未来任务的下游影响来优化筛选决策。跨越多样化基准和 LLM 骨干，SkillOS 始终同时提升性能与效率。进一步分析表明，经过训练的技能筛选能力可超越前沿模型的零样本筛选能力，并可在不同设置间泛化，凸显模块化、经训练的技能筛选是通向能从经验中自我进化智能体的一条实用路径。

                                                                                                                                              7. Acknowledgments

                                                                                                                                              A. Prompts

                                                                                                                                              A. 提示词

                                                                                                                                              In this section, we provide the full prompt templates used throughout different phases and components of our framework. 在本节中，我们将给出整个框架在不同阶段与各组件中所使用的完整提示词模板。

                                                                                                                                              A.1. Prompt for Skill Curator

                                                                                                                                              A.1. 技能策展人提示
